title: Agent Traffic Report: 23% of News Site Visits Now Come from AI Systems
author: DataTech Research Team
summary: Comprehensive analysis of AI agent traffic patterns across 200+ news sites. Agent visits grew 8x in 12 months, with research agents dominating at 61% of agent traffic.
content:
AI Agent Traffic Analysis — May 2026

Methodology: Analysis of server logs from 214 news and media websites, representing 4.2B monthly page views. Agent identification via User-Agent strings, behavioral patterns (request timing, navigation paths), and x402 payment headers.

Headline Numbers:
- 23% of all visits now come from AI agents (up from 3% in May 2025)
- 61% are research/analysis agents (enterprise tools gathering information)
- 24% are content aggregation agents (building summaries, newsletters)
- 11% are monitoring agents (tracking topics, competitors, mentions)
- 4% are commerce agents (price comparison, product research)

Traffic Patterns:
- Agent traffic peaks at 02:00-06:00 UTC (off-peak for humans, optimal for batch processing)
- Average agent session: 12 pages in 4.2 seconds (vs. human: 3.1 pages in 4.5 minutes)
- Agent bounce rate: 89% on paywalled content (vs. 34% for humans)
- Agent conversion on x402-enabled sites: 72% (agents that encounter a 402 and have budget will pay)

Revenue Impact:
- Sites with x402: $0.004 average revenue per agent visit
- Sites without x402: $0.00 per agent visit (100% unmonetized)
- Top-performing publisher: $47,000/month from agent payments alone (financial data provider)
- Median publisher with x402: $3,200/month from agent payments

Agent Identity Distribution:
- Enterprise research tools (dedicated search agents, custom corporate agents): 41%
- Developer tools (code assistants accessing docs): 18%
- Marketing/SEO agents: 14%
- Financial analysis agents: 12%
- Academic research agents: 9%
- Other/unidentified: 6%

Key Insight: The 72% conversion rate on x402-enabled sites proves that agents WILL pay when given the option. The current 89% bounce rate on paywalled sites represents pure lost revenue — these agents have budgets and willingness to pay, but no mechanism to do so.
